rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO BUENO ET AL VS. HAWK LING LOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Hawk Lou, Ketty Lou, Hawk Lou'S Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of Previously Deposed Plaintiffs Or Their Persons Most Knowledgeable And Request For Sanctions Against Each Plaintiff And Their Attorney, Michael Schreiber In The Amount Of $7,206.00.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 19, 2017, line 1. Defendants Hawk Lou, Ketty Lou, Hawk Lou'S Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of Previously Deposed Plaintiffs Or Their Persons Most Knowledgeable And Request For Sanctions Against Each Plaintiff And Their Attorney, Michael Schreiber In The Amount Of $7,206.00. GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendants' motion to compel further deposition testimony is GRANTED. Plaintiff's counsel's instructions not to answer were improper. Relevant depositions are re-opened only with respect to the topics that Defendants were previously obstructed from receiving information about. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiffs' counsel in the amount of $5046. Defendants' request for Plaintiffs to pay for future court reporter fees and costs associated with 12 additional hours of deposition time is DENIED. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ABERNATHY VS. CITYWIDE COUNCIL SENIOR DISABLED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Citywide Council Senior Disabled And Beverly Saba'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 19, 2017, line 6. Defendants Citywide Council Senior Disabled And Beverly Saba'S Demurrer To Complaint SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff lacks standing and fails to state a cause of action. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN GOLD VS. WILLIAM WATTS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 19, 2017, line 7. DEFENDANT WILLIAM WATTS' Motion To Continue Trial GRANTED pursuant to CRC 3.1332(c)(3). Trial is continued to June 5, 2017. The MSC is continued to May 31, 2017. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: A. IVANA MATTHEWS VS. PAUL WOODBERRY
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 19, 2017, line 5. PLAINTIFF A. IVANA MATTHEWS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Plaintiff has met her burden of establishing a prima facie case for an unlawful detainer action under CCP 1161a (b)(3). = (501/REQ)
